title: A Calendar Of Sonnets - August
content:
Silence again. The glorious symphony
    Hath need of pause and interval of peace.
    Some subtle signal bids all sweet sounds cease,
    Save hum of insects' aimless industry.
    Pathetic summer seeks by blazonry
    Of color to conceal her swift decrease.
    Weak subterfuge! Each mocking day doth fleece
    A blossom, and lay bare her poverty.
    Poor middle-ag�d summer! Vain this show!
    Whole fields of golden-rod cannot offset
    One meadow with a single violet;
    And well the singing thrush and lily know,
    Spite of all artifice which her regret
    Can deck in splendid guise, their time to go!
meaning:
Helen Hunt Jackson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "A Calendar Of Sonnets - August"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
